content.text:
```json
{
  "module": "core-workflows",
  "scores": {
    "visual_polish": 52,
    "domain_depth": 48,
    "data_richness": 40,
    "interaction_quality": 44,
    "ai_integration": 25,
    "mobile_first": 32,
    "performance": 50,
    "typescript_quality": 55,
    "error_handling": 42,
    "realtime": 35,
    "accessibility": 28,
    "security": 45,
    "differentiation": 30,
    "enterprise_readiness": 38
  },
  "overall_score": 39,
  "issues": [
    {
      "id": "core-workflows-C1-001",
      "severity": "critical",
      "dimension": "accessibility",
      "title": "RFI table rows lack keyboard navigation and ARIA roles",
      "file": "src/pages/RFIs.tsx",
      "description": "The RFI list table does not use semantic table markup with role='table', role='row', role='columnheader'. Rows are not focusable or keyboard navigable. Screen readers cannot parse the data grid. PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA requires axe-core zero critical violations and keyboard navigable tab order.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/pages/RFIs.tsx, in the main table/list rendering section: BEFORE: table rows are rendered as div elements or tr elements without ARIA attributes, no tabIndex, no onKeyDown handler for Enter to open detail. AFTER: Add role='table' to the container, role='rowgroup' to thead/tbody, role='row' to each tr/row div, role='columnheader' to header cells, role='cell' to data cells. Add tabIndex={0} to each data row and an onKeyDown handler that opens the RFI detail when Enter is pressed. Add aria-label='RFI list' to the table container. Add aria-sort attributes to sortable column headers. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "core-workflows-C1-002",
      "severity": "critical",
      "dimension": "error_handling",
      "title": "Submittals page has no loading skeleton or error state",
      "file": "src/pages/Submittals.tsx",
      "description": "The Submittals page shows no skeleton loader during data fetch and no error state on fetch failure. PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA Global Standards require skeleton loaders matching final layout within 100ms and inline retry on error, never a blank screen.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/pages/Submittals.tsx, in the main component function: BEFORE: The component renders the table immediately or shows nothing while isLoading is true, and when error occurs there is no visible error UI. AFTER: When isLoading is true, render a skeleton loader with 5 rows matching the table column layout (use pulsing div placeholders at 48px row height with border-radius 4px and background theme.colors.border, animated with opacity 0.3 to 0.7). When error is truthy, render an inline error card with the message 'Unable to load submittals. Check your connection and try again.' and a 'Retry' button that calls refetch(). Wrap the skeleton in a container matching the table's grid template columns. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "core-workflows-C1-003",
      "severity": "critical",
      "dimension": "mobile_first",
      "title": "Change Orders table overflows on mobile with no responsive fallback",
      "file": "src/pages/ChangeOrders.tsx",
      "description": "The Change Orders page renders a wide multi-column table that causes horizontal overflow on screens below 768px. PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA requires every page usable at 375px width with no horizontal scroll. Tables must collapse to card layout or provide frozen first column on mobile.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/pages/ChangeOrders.tsx, in the main component function where the table is rendered: BEFORE: The table renders all columns (CO#, Title, Type, Status, Amount, Impact, Date) at all viewport widths, causing horizontal overflow on mobile. AFTER: Add a responsive wrapper. When window.innerWidth < 768 (use a useEffect with resize listener or a CSS media query via inline style check), render change orders as stacked cards instead of table rows. Each card shows: CO number and title as header, status badge, amount formatted as currency, and type as a tag. Keep the table layout for desktop (>=768px). Add overflow-x auto with WebkitOverflowScrolling 'touch' as a fallback for tablets. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "core-workflows-C1-004",
      "severity": "major",
      "dimension": "interaction_quality",
      "title": "Punch List has no empty state when filters return zero results",
      "file": "src/pages/PunchList.tsx",
      "description": "When filters are applied and no punch items match, the page shows a blank area or the same empty state as true-empty. PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA requires filtered empty states to differ from true-empty: 'No results match your filters' with a 'Clear filters' action button.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/pages/PunchList.tsx, in the section where filtered results are rendered: BEFORE: When the data array is empty after filtering, either nothing is shown or the generic 'No punch list items' empty state appears regardless of whether filters are active. AFTER: Track whether any filter is active (check if status filter, trade filter, assignee filter, or search query is non-default). If filters are active AND data is empty, render a distinct filtered-empty state: an icon (Search or Filter icon from lucide-react), text 'No punch items match your current filters', and a 'Clear All Filters' button that resets all filter state variables. If no filters are active AND data is empty, show the true empty state with the existing illustration and 'Create First Punch Item' CTA. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "core-workflows-C1-005",
      "severity": "major",
      "dimension": "domain_depth",
      "title": "RFI detail missing Ball-in-Court indicator and days open calculation",
      "file": "src/pages/RFIs.tsx",
      "description": "The RFI list and detail view do not show a Ball-in-Court (BIC) indicator or computed Days Open value. PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA for RFIs requires BIC color-coded by party and Days Open calculated as today minus created_at for open RFIs. These are core construction PM metrics that every GC checks daily.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/pages/RFIs.tsx, in the table row rendering and any detail panel: BEFORE: Rows show status and priority but no Ball-in-Court column and no Days Open column. AFTER: Add a 'Days Open' column that calculates Math.floor((Date.now() - new Date(rfi.created_at).getTime()) / 86400000) for open/under_review RFIs, and for closed RFIs calculates using closed_at minus created_at. Display as an integer. Add a 'Ball in Court' column that shows the assigned_to name (or 'Unassigned') with a small colored dot: blue (#3B82F6) for GC, purple (#8B5CF6) for Architect, teal (#14B8A6) for Engineer, orange (#F47820) for Owner. If assigned_to is null, show gray (#6B7280) dot with 'Unassigned'. For overdue RFIs (due_date < today AND status not closed), add a red 'OVERDUE' badge next to the due date cell. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    }
  ],
  "invented_features": [],
  "competitive_intel": "Procore's RFI module has Ball-in-Court tracking, email reply from architects, and response time analytics as table-stakes features. Fieldwire has basic RFI/submittal but no financial linkage. SiteSync's core-workflows module currently lacks BIC indicators, days-open calculations, filtered empty states, and mobile responsiveness — all features Procore ships out of the box. The accessibility gaps would also fail enterprise procurement accessibility audits that firms like Turner and Skanska require.",
  "summary": "The core-workflows module (RFIs, Submittals, Change Orders, Punch List) has a functional data layer with typed API endpoints and Supabase integration, but falls short on construction domain depth and UI completeness. Critical gaps include missing Ball-in-Court tracking on RFIs (a daily-use metric for every PM), absent skeleton loaders and error states on Submittals, table overflow on Change Orders at mobile widths, no differentiated filtered-empty state on Punch List, and zero ARIA attributes on the RFI table making it inaccessible to screen readers. These five issues directly violate PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA numbered test cases and represent the highest-impact fixes for this module in Surgeon mode."
}
```
